quests.title: Quest 1: Lottery Scam
quests.subtitle: The Surprise Call
quests.description: A caller claims you won a huge lottery you never entered. Spot the trick before excitement takes over.

reward.title: Smart Decision Maker 🧠
reward.description: You learned how to slow down, verify claims, and avoid upfront-fee prize scams.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: (Phone rings 📱)

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: Congratulations! You’ve won a lottery of ₹10,00,000!

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: Wait… I never entered any lottery…

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: It’s part of a promotional draw. Your number was selected randomly.

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.text: ₹10 lakh?? That’s insane, you’re so lucky!

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: This feels… too good to be true.

choices.text: Really?? How do I claim it?
choices.outcome_hint: Curiosity is natural, but excitement can make scams feel convincing.

choices.text: I don’t remember entering any lottery…
choices.outcome_hint: Good pause. Verifying the basic claim is the first defense.

choices.text: This sounds suspicious, I’m not interested.
choices.outcome_hint: Strong boundary. Scams lose power when you refuse pressure.

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: To release your prize, you just need to pay a small processing fee of ₹3,000.

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: Once paid, your winnings will be transferred immediately.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: Why do I have to pay to receive money?

choices.text: Okay, I’ll pay ₹3,000. ❌
choices.outcome_hint: Upfront fees for prizes are a major red flag.

choices.text: Can you share official proof or website?
choices.outcome_hint: Asking for official verification slows the scam down.

choices.text: I don’t trust this, I won’t pay.
choices.outcome_hint: Correct instinct. Real prizes do not need advance payment.

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: This offer is valid only for today. If you don’t act now, the prize will be cancelled.

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.text: Just pay it quickly, what if it’s real?

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: Why are they rushing me…

choices.text: Pay quickly before time runs out. ❌
choices.outcome_hint: Urgency is designed to stop you from thinking clearly.

choices.text: Take time and verify the information.
choices.outcome_hint: Taking time is one of the best scam defenses.

choices.text: Ignore and disconnect the call.
choices.outcome_hint: Ending suspicious calls protects you from more pressure.

scenes.speaker: System
scenes.title: Mini Quiz Mode 🎯
scenes.prompt: Identify what seems wrong in this situation:
scenes.feedback_correct: Good observation. These are common scam patterns.
scenes.feedback_retry: Look carefully — something isn’t right here.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: Real prizes don’t ask for money…

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: Scammers often use excitement and urgency to trick people.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: I almost believed it.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: The caller is no longer reachable.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: I lost money…

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: Always verify before trusting such offers.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: You avoided a scam.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: That was clearly fake.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: Being cautious protects your money.

scenes.speaker: System
scenes.title: Key Takeaways:
takeaways: If you didn’t enter, you didn’t win
Real lotteries don’t ask for upfront fees
Urgency is often a scam tactic

quests.title: Quest 2: Fake Loan Call
quests.subtitle: Scam Alert – Fake Loan Calls
quests.description: An instant loan caller offers fast money with no documents. Decide what is real and what is bait.

reward.title: Scam Awareness Increased 🔐
reward.description: You learned to protect personal data and reject upfront-fee loan traps.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: (Phone rings 📱)

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: Hello ma’am/sir, congratulations! You’ve been pre-approved for an instant loan of ₹2,00,000.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: I didn’t even apply for a loan…

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: No problem! This is a special offer. No documents, no waiting.

choices.text: Sounds good, tell me more.
choices.outcome_hint: Listening further can be okay, but stay alert when you never applied.

choices.text: How did you get my number?
choices.outcome_hint: Good question. Legitimate lenders should explain how they contacted you.

choices.text: I’m not interested, please don’t call again.
choices.outcome_hint: Clear refusal protects your attention and personal data.

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: This offer is only valid today. You must act fast.

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: Just pay a small processing fee of ₹2,000 to activate your loan.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: Why do I need to pay before getting the loan?

choices.text: Okay, I’ll pay the fee. ❌
choices.outcome_hint: Upfront processing fees on cold calls are a common scam signal.

choices.text: Can you send official details on email?
choices.outcome_hint: Asking for verifiable official details is safer than paying immediately.

choices.text: This sounds suspicious, I won’t proceed.
choices.outcome_hint: Good boundary. Pressure plus fees should stop the conversation.

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: To proceed, share your Aadhaar number and bank details.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: That feels risky…

choices.text: Share details quickly to get the loan. ❌
choices.outcome_hint: Never share sensitive identity or bank details on an unverified call.

choices.text: Ask for company verification first.
choices.outcome_hint: Verification protects your identity and money.

choices.text: Refuse and end the call.
choices.outcome_hint: Ending the call is the safest move when personal data is requested.

scenes.speaker: System
scenes.title: Mini Quiz Mode 🎯
scenes.prompt: Identify the red flags in this situation:
scenes.feedback_correct: Good awareness. These are common scam signs.
scenes.feedback_retry: Take another look — something doesn’t feel right here.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: This doesn’t feel like a genuine offer.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: Scam calls often create urgency and ask for money or personal data.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: I’m glad I didn’t rush into it.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: You paid ₹2,000 as a fee.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: The caller is no longer reachable.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: I got scammed…

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: Always verify before trusting such offers.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: You avoided a potential scam.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: That was close.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: Being cautious helps protect your money.

scenes.speaker: System
scenes.title: Key Takeaways:
takeaways: Never pay upfront fees for loans
Avoid sharing personal/bank details on calls

quests.title: Quest 3: Fake Stock Investment Tip
quests.subtitle: Hot Tip or Trap?
quests.description: A stock tip promises fast guaranteed returns. Check whether the opportunity is wisdom or a trap.

reward.title: Smart Investor Mindset 📊
reward.description: You learned to question guaranteed returns and verify investment advice before acting.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: (Phone rings 📱)

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: Hello, I’m calling from a stock advisory firm.

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: We have an exclusive tip — a stock that will double in 2 weeks.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: Double? That sounds too fast…

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: Our VIP clients are already investing. You don’t want to miss this.

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.text: Bro if it doubles, just invest na!

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: I don’t know… this feels risky.

choices.text: Tell me more about the stock.
choices.outcome_hint: Curiosity is fine, but guaranteed fast profits need extra caution.

choices.text: Is your company registered?
choices.outcome_hint: Registration and verification matter before trusting financial advice.

choices.text: I’m not interested.
choices.outcome_hint: Refusing unknown tips is a valid protective move.

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: You just need to invest ₹10,000 now.

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: We guarantee returns — minimum 2x profit.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: Guaranteed returns in stock market?

choices.text: Okay, I’ll invest ₹10,000. ❌
choices.outcome_hint: Guaranteed stock returns are a strong investment scam signal.

choices.text: Can you share official proof and registration?
choices.outcome_hint: Demanding proof helps filter out fake advisors.

choices.text: No one can guarantee returns, I won’t invest.
choices.outcome_hint: Correct. Markets involve risk and no genuine advisor can remove it.

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: This is a limited-time opportunity.

lines.speaker: Scammer
lines.text: If you delay, you’ll miss the profit.

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.text: Yaar just try once, what’s the harm?

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: Why are they rushing me…

choices.text: Invest quickly before it’s too late. ❌
choices.outcome_hint: Pressure to rush an investment is a red flag.

choices.text: Take time and research the stock.
choices.outcome_hint: Research gives you control instead of letting pressure decide.

choices.text: Ignore and disconnect.
choices.outcome_hint: Disconnecting from an unverified advisor is a smart move.

scenes.speaker: System
scenes.title: Mini Quiz Mode 🎯
scenes.feedback_correct: Good thinking. These are common investment scam signals.
scenes.feedback_retry: Look closely — something doesn’t add up.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: Real investments don’t promise guaranteed profit…

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: The stock market involves risk. No one can guarantee returns.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: I almost rushed into it.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: ₹10,000 invested via unknown source.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: No returns received. Contact lost.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: I got tricked…

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: Always verify before investing.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: You avoided a risky investment scam.

lines.speaker: Adam/Eve
lines.text: Good thing I didn’t rush.

lines.speaker: System
lines.text: Smart decisions protect your money.

scenes.speaker: System
scenes.title: Key Takeaways:
takeaways: No one can guarantee stock market returns
